reviews.review_msg: Amazing class and prof. I got to read about mythology every week! Need to keep up with schedule and weekly readings though. Saw others struggle trying to study material just before quizzes. Hd questions about assignments and marking which she answered in email and during office hrs. Fun elective but get ready to read!!

She is sweet, approachable, and funny. The material in this course was very interesting. But it was pretty hard for a 102 level course, each week I felt overwhelmed and could never finish my readings on time, the criteria for assignments was difficult to understand and her grading was harsh. But her quiz and final were easy.

made us read billion pages of reading every week and marked all our work the day she submitted our grades so we can't even argue and don't even know what we did wrong. her grading is so insanly harsh for no reason, and never gave us proper feedback. don't take this class. save yourself. i regret not dropping

i don't know how she got these high reviews, i took this class as an ELECTIVE and somehow it's my worst class and i'm a science major. her marking is so insanely tough, she makes us write essays and never gives us clear examples or what to expect. she takes marks off for literally everything for no reason and doesn't give clear feedback.

Dense lectures that are half recap from the previous week's lecture and sometimes a few bits from before. Kind and supportive, but very tough marker and took a long time to return assignments. We were given around 50 pages of reading to do per week, but was easy to fall behind in.

Took 102 as my elective, it became my most stressful/hard course +the lowest grade I received this semester. The grading section on canvas was turned off and I received most of my assignments the last week of the semester so it was very hard to estimate what grade I was at. And the work was marked insanely hard with little to no comments as to why

The course is pretty chill, and easy to keep track with new material. The homework was not too hard, class participation matters and assignments were very manageable, although some of her grading criteria can be difficult to follow at times. Nice GPA booster.

Her grading is harsh, her rubrics for written assignments do not make any sense, save your GPA and do not take a class with her. Her teaching style is disorganized, takes a while to grade stuff, I could never figure out what she wanted us to talk about in the writing assignments. She will always find something wrong with what you wrote.
probably one of the worst courses i've ever taken.

I'm honestly very surprised her ratings are so high because HUM 102 was probably one of the worst courses I've ever taken. Her grading is extremely harsh, and this is coming from someone who gets good grades. Her assignment criteria is disorganized and impossible to find, and the entire course was all over the place. Grading is also very very slow.

Took HUM102W w/ her, I fear it was her first time teaching the course. Grading criteria for written assignments made no sense, lectures were all over the place and she would spend half of each lecture reviewing last week's content, tests were easy but portfolio marking was brutal with limited feedback and delayed grade posting.

I truly enjoyed being in this class; I especially appreciated it when she got us timbits weekly! Her lectures involve discussing historical women and their heroinism, and I did write 3 essays and gained good feedback. For fairness, I would like her to prioritize letting someone who did not yet speak to share their point for in-class participation.

Awesome prof! She would extend our deadlines for assignments to prevent us from burning out. She did buy the class timbits almost every week to cheer us up, for real! It would have been better if she didn't make us borrow or buy some readings, though. Again, so far she has been a very nice prof!

One of the best professors I've taken at sfu - super interesting, was accommodating regarding office hours (she made appointments when people couldn't make her stated hours), gave extensions out like candy. i will say that participation DOES matter!! dropped my A to an A- but overall she is amazing!! HIGHLY recommend!!

I had the best of time in her class; she is caring for her students, and loves what she teaches. I do think she gives out A LOT of readings, but most often from larger books she only picks out chapters and not the whole book. She's flexible with due dates as long as it's reasonable, so I guess it's a fair compromise.
